title: <div>$AUTOMATION_ENGINE – the orchestrator that responds to events and runs auto
content:
$AUTOMATION_ENGINE – the orchestrator that responds to events and runs automations.

Each root is governed by specific AI roles.

An “Architect” role can modify $OS_ROOT specs and conventions.
An “Archivist” role writes to $ARCHIVE_ROOT and maintains indices.
A “Prompt-Smith” role curates $PROMPT_ROOT.
A “Builder” role operates in $REPO_ROOT, emitting code or structured assets.
A “Conductor” role configures $AUTOMATION_ENGINE to wire events and jobs.

These roles can be embodied by different AIs, human collaborators, or both; what matters is that their responsibilities are clearly partitioned.

2.2 $OS_ROOT Structure

$OS_ROOT should be treated as the conceptual kernel. At minimum it contains:

$OS_ROOT/config
$OS_ROOT/specs
$OS_ROOT/worlds
$OS_ROOT/agents
$OS_ROOT/meta

$OS_ROOT/config: base configuration, including environment variable templates, global constants, and naming-scheme definitions.
